seoTitle: How to Use Segments in Brevo Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to build multi-criteria Brevo segments using attribute and behavioral filters, then deploy them directly into a live campaign.
intro: Blasting your entire list with the same message tends to hurt open rates and increase unsubscribes over time. This guide shows how to build targeted segments in Brevo using attribute filters like location or signup date, combined with behavioral filters like email opens or clicks. You'll also learn how to save a segment and pull it directly into a campaign for precise targeting.

steps.heading: Open the Segments tab
steps.body: Go to Contacts, then click the Segments tab located above your contact database grid.

steps.heading: Create a new segment
steps.body: Click Create a segment and then Add a condition to open the list of filtering attributes and tracking criteria.

steps.heading: Apply attribute-based filters
steps.body: Choose Contact attributes, such as Subscription date, and set your desired time value to filter fresh signups.

steps.heading: Layer in behavioral filters
steps.body: Click Add an 'AND' condition, then choose Email tracking, Opened, and Any campaign to combine engagement data with your attribute rule.

steps.heading: Save and deploy the segment
steps.body: Save the segment with a clear name, then in a new campaign, switch the recipient view from Lists to Segments to select it directly.

faq.question: Do Brevo segments update automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, segments run continuous background tracking, so the contact pool updates automatically as subscriber behavior changes.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple filter conditions in one segment?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use AND/OR logic to combine attribute-based and behavioral filters for more precise targeting.

faq.question: How do I use a saved segment in a campaign?
faq.answer: In the campaign recipient step, switch the target list drawer from Lists to Segments to select your saved filter.

faq.question: What's the difference between a list and a segment?
faq.answer: A list is a static group you manually assign contacts to, while a segment is a dynamic filter that automatically updates based on the rules you set.
